posts.name: Rob Sutherland
posts.cooked:
Has anyone had it? Thoughts?

1985 Charlie is my favorite champagne of all time and I’ve been lucky enough to have had it numerous times as well as all of the other 80’s renditions (which I loved but not to the outsized, fanatical degree of the '85). How does the new release stack up? I have an offer at $700/bottle which is eye watering but also I guess understandable. Is it available anywhere else for less?

From the offer I got:
As cellarmaster Cyril Brun noted, “Champagne Charlie is an overwhelming multi-vintage, a quest for the ultimate free absolute. For this new edition of Charlie, I took my inspiration from previous editions. My first impression of these wines were overwhelming: the freshness and aromatic intensity, the silky saline finish, could be systematically found in all the wines as if these qualities transcended the effect of the vintage. It was the key!”

The result from the mind of our cellarmaster is a blend of 52% Chardonnay and 48% Pinot Noir. Close to 80% of the blend was created from older reserve wines that date back decades, adding complexity and richness to the layers of flavor in the cuvée. The remaining 20% is made from the 2016 vintage, all from Grand Cru vineyards that the House has collaborated with for generations. The wine was then assembled and cellared in 2017.

posts.name: Guillermo M e n a
posts.cooked: I don’t know that I’d ever buy any wine with a description that includes the word “overwhelming,” let alone twice.

posts.name: Jon Lawrence
posts.cooked: I had it at the Champagne Charlie release party in NYC. I enjoyed it, but I also enjoyed the Charles Heidsieck NV they were pouring beforehand. The Charlie is bit more complex with some nutty, toasted almonds, marzipan flavors coming from the older vintages, but it is still very fresh thanks to the '16. I imagine they tried to price this based on limited quantities, great provenance, history, etc. Despite these things, without any professional tasting notes claiming this is the best $700 Champagne out there, I think this will be a tough sell.

posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked:
I got the same offer. I read it too, knowing they were burying the lead.

I mean, come on. Really?

I was disappointed by the “if you have to ask” nature of this pricing strategy. From a house I respect.
Well this is easy…7-8 bottles of the Brut Millésimé

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked: Well…I thought…Charlie Fu and his new IG video wine reviews…and now…He’s making a Champagne?!?! Guy’s exploding!!

posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked:
Well…I thought…Charlie Fu and his new IG video wine reviews…and now…He’s making a Champagne?!?! Guy’s exploding!!

Charlie on Instagram: "Love love love D’auvenay. If you don’t - head to your local Otolaryngologist asap. ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀ 2011 Dauvenay meursault - 288 bottles produced. Blend of Narvaux, Chaumes, Pre de Manche in low yield vintages. flint sweet corn yellow peach. Really dense yellow fruit and richness is so incredible. There’s a touch of opulent honeyed fruit and mineralogy. the palate is long and expansive. I wonder where it ages? It’s golden but perfect. An absolute treat - wish I could drink D’auvenay every day. Too bad I didn’t win the mega millions."

When I first saw one of these the other day, I thought that Charlie was a free-floating Baron Harkonnen.

I think they’re great! Especially with the wines he drinks!! Next up…maybe live vid notes?

posts.name: Scott Brunson

Has anyone had it? Thoughts?

1985 Charlie is my favorite champagne of all time and I’ve been lucky enough to have had it numerous times as well as all of the other 80’s renditions (which I loved but not to the outsized, fanatical degree of the '85).

I have one precious bottle remaining (wedding year).

posts.name: Hank Victor
posts.cooked: Big fan of Charles Heidsieck but the $700 price tag is hard to swallow. I’ve also been disappointed with some of their Le Crayeres collection bottles and would rather purchase multiple btls of BdM.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: It’s a very nice wine, with real fleshy plenitude and reserve wine complexity. $700 seems quite hard to digest, however.

posts.name: Mikko Tuomi
posts.cooked:
“ultimate free absolute”.
Some copywriter was getting paid by the word there.
